title: anthyllis leguminosa var. coccinea
intro: Anthyllis leguminosa var. coccinea, also known as red kidney vetch or red anthyllis, is a perennial legume plant native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, characterized by its red flowers and kidney-shaped leaves. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high content of phenolic compounds and flavonoids. The health benefits of red anthyllis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system, as well as its use as a natural remedy for digestive issues and skin problems. Red anthyllis can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in herbal supplements and topical creams, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
